sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.content: The simulation of 68 agents across Twitter and Reddit platforms over 100 rounds revealed a generally positive reception to the Developer Productivity Dashboard concept. 72% of simulated engineering managers expressed strong interest, while developers showed mixed reactions regarding privacy concerns.

sections.title: Sentiment Analysis
sections.content: Overall sentiment: Positive (72% favorable). Engineering managers showed the strongest positive response (85% favorable). Developers were more cautious (58% favorable), with primary concerns around surveillance perception and metric gaming. Tech leads fell in between (68% favorable).

sections.title: Key Concerns and Risks
sections.content: 1. Privacy perception: 42% of developer agents raised concerns about feeling monitored. Recommendation: Position as team health tool, not individual tracking. 2. Metric gaming: 28% of agents predicted developers would optimize for dashboard metrics rather than actual productivity. 3. Tool fatigue: 15% expressed reluctance to adopt another dashboard tool. 4. Data accuracy: Questions about whether aggregated metrics truly reflect productivity.

sections.title: Feature Reception Rankings
sections.content: Features ranked by simulated reception: 1. Burnout early warning system (reception: 0.89) - strongest positive response across all personas. 2. Automated standup reports (reception: 0.78) - managers loved it, some developers found it reductive. 3. Team health dashboard (reception: 0.72). 4. Cross-platform aggregation (reception: 0.65). 5. CI/CD integration (reception: 0.55) - lowest interest, seen as table stakes.

sections.title: Recommendations
sections.content: Should prioritize burnout detection as the hero feature. Must address privacy concerns in onboarding and messaging. Consider making individual metrics opt-in rather than default. Should de-emphasize CI/CD integration in initial launch. Must differentiate from existing tools like LinearB and Jellyfish.

sections.title: Notable Agent Reactions
sections.content: Agent EM_014 (Engineering Manager): 'Finally, something that could replace my Monday morning status meeting ritual.' Agent DEV_042 (Senior Developer): 'I would actively resist this unless I control what data is shared.' Agent TL_007 (Tech Lead): 'The burnout detection alone would justify the cost.' Agent DEV_089 (Junior Developer): 'Feels like my manager is watching my every commit.' Agent PM_023 (Product Manager): 'Would love to see how sprint velocity correlates with the health metrics.'
